Australian Federal Elections (2004–2026) in 4D Simplex Space

Inspired by ABC News Ternary Plot analysis, expanding into a WebGL 4-dimensional simplex across 8 federal elections

Key Vertices (100% Corners)

Labor (ALP): (0,0,0) bottom-left ground
Coalition (LNP): (1,0,0) bottom-right ground
One Nation (ONP): Top vertical 3D elevation peak
Other / Greens / Teals: Top-back ground corner

3D Navigation Controls:
• Rotate: Left-click & drag on canvas
• Zoom: Mouse wheel scroll
• Pan: Right-click & drag

Project Citations & Data Sources

• Inspiration: ABC News Interactive Ternary Analysis,
"Over five decades, here's how voters have shifted away from the major parties" (April 2025).

• Official AEC Data: Certified division-by-division candidate primary vote downloads for 2004, 2007, 2010, 2013, 2016, 2019, 2022, and 2025 (results.aec.gov.au).

• Psephos Archive: Dr Adam Carr's historical electoral database (psephos.adam-carr.net).

• 2026 Polling Consensus: National tracking from YouGov (yougov.com), Roy Morgan (roymorgan.com), RedBridge, and Antony Green's electoral commentary (antonygreen.com.au).

One Nation Focus: 2025 Exact Actuals vs 2026 Polling Surge

National Primary Shift: Surged from 6.40% (2025 Actual) to 25.0% - 32.0% (Early 2026 YouGov/RedBridge/Morgan polls).
Key 2026 Lore Hardpoints:
• ★ Farrer (NSW) Victory: David Farley won the May 2026 by-election with exactly 39.54% primary (57.5% 2CP), taking the seat from LNP.
• ★ New England (NSW) Stronghold: Barnaby Joyce defected in Dec 2025 (modeled at 55% ONP base).
